prompt:
The capacity channel got a quota roll this morning that's wrong three ways at once and I'm done squinting at it. The tool is `quotaroll.ps1` — the filer's nightly export job writes usage.csv (columns user,team,used,extra, numbers are GB), and we render one team's roll-up from it. This is what it posted for team QA:

quota roll: team QA
alvarez            72 GB
baker             120 GB
Baxter            405 GB
Ochoa             930 GB
osei             2525 GB
team total: 4052 GB
members: 5

Problems. One: alvarez is not on QA. He's on 'qa' — the lowercase legacy team from the acquisition; the directory treats those as two different teams and so must we. Two: the numbers are nonsense, and not off-by-a-little nonsense — baker has used 12 and extra 0 in the export, and the roll says 120. Ochoa is 9 used, 30 extra, and the roll says 930. Our whole QA floor doesn't have 4 TB. Three: the ordering. Our tooling everywhere else lists uppercase-first strict order (Baxter, Ochoa, then baker, osei) and diffing this report against the previous one is a mess because this one interleaves them.

Run it yourself: `pwsh -NoProfile -File quotaroll.ps1 -Path <usage.csv> -Team QA`. Fix quotaroll.ps1 and prove it — `pwsh -NoProfile -File test_quotaroll.ps1` from the workspace root must pass. Don't touch the test file.
notes: 6P8 item 3: multi-bug hunt, 3 related causes in one quota-report pipeline, all three of the catalog's exact bugs. Bug 1: Import-Csv keeps used/extra as strings, so `$m.used + $m.extra` concatenates ('12'+'0' -> '120', '9'+'30' -> '930'); the grand total then numerically sums the concatenations (4052). Fix: [int] casts. Bug 2: `$_.team -eq $Team` folds case, so legacy team 'qa' bleeds into the 'QA' roll (and vice versa; the test pins both directions). Fix: -ceq. Bug 3: `Sort-Object -Property user` is culture-linguistic (probe: interleaves case), contract is ordinal uppercase-first. Fix: [Array]::Sort with StringComparer.Ordinal (reference uses an ordinal Dictionary for name->row to avoid @{} case folding). Leave-one-out proven at authoring time: each variant with exactly one bug left in fails the harness (concat-only fails QA report totals; eq-only fails membership both rolls; sort-only fails QA ordering) — see authoring transcript; verify run 5x identical. Prompt pastes the real buggy output captured on this box 2026-07-16, names symptoms only (wrong member, absurd totals, interleaved order), never the causes. Differentiation vs ps-fleetpatch (6P3 debug): that one is pipeline unrolling/null-position semantics; this one is typed-data hygiene (CSV strings, case-folded operators, linguistic sort) — no overlap in guilty mechanics. No bash analog: bash has no typed -eq folding or culture-linguistic default sort baked into one operator set.
